challenges.name: Ruck It Up
challenges.one_liner: Ruck 3x per week with 20-40lb for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Loaded carries improve bone density, metabolic conditioning, and grip strength
challenges.board_quote: Rucking is Zone 2 with a strength stimulus. Two adaptations for the price of one.
challenges.protocol: 3 walks per week with 20-40lb ruck. Minimum 30 minutes each.

challenges.name: 10K Steps
challenges.one_liner: Hit 10,000 steps every single day for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: 10,000 steps/day associated with 40-50% reduction in all-cause mortality
challenges.board_quote: The simplest longevity intervention that exists.
challenges.protocol: 10,000 steps minimum per day. Auto-verified from Garmin/Apple Health.

challenges.name: Morning Light Walk
challenges.one_liner: Outdoor walk within 30 min of waking for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Morning sunlight resets circadian rhythm, improves cortisol awakening response
challenges.board_quote: Morning sunlight within 30 minutes of waking is the single highest-ROI protocol.
challenges.protocol: Walk outdoors within 30 min of waking. Minimum 10 min. No sunglasses.

challenges.name: Run a 5K
challenges.one_liner: Complete a 5K run without stopping
challenges.evidence_summary: VO2max is the strongest predictor of all-cause mortality
challenges.board_quote: Building cardiovascular capacity is non-negotiable for the centenarian decathlon.
challenges.protocol: Complete a 5K (3.1 miles) running continuously.

challenges.name: 60-Second Dead Hang
challenges.one_liner: Hang from a bar for 60 seconds without dropping
challenges.evidence_summary: Grip strength is one of the strongest predictors of all-cause mortality
challenges.board_quote: Centenarian decathlon benchmark.
challenges.protocol: Hang from a pull-up bar with both hands for 60 continuous seconds.

challenges.name: Always Take the Stairs
challenges.one_liner: No elevators or escalators for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Stair climbing associated with reduced cardiovascular mortality
challenges.board_quote: The easiest way to add NEAT to your day.
challenges.protocol: Take stairs every time. Zero elevator or escalator use.

challenges.name: 20 Consecutive Pushups
challenges.one_liner: Complete 20 full pushups without stopping
challenges.evidence_summary: Pushup capacity inversely associated with cardiovascular events
challenges.board_quote: Simple, measurable, functional strength benchmark.
challenges.protocol: 20 full pushups, chest to ground, no pausing.

challenges.name: Screens Off
challenges.one_liner: No screens 1 hour before bed for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Blue light suppresses melatonin by up to 50%
challenges.board_quote: The single most impactful sleep hygiene change most people can make.
challenges.protocol: All screens off 60 minutes before target bedtime.

challenges.name: Clockwork Sleep
challenges.one_liner: Same bedtime ±30 min for 14 straight days
challenges.evidence_summary: Irregular sleep timing increases cardiovascular risk
challenges.board_quote: Social jetlag is the silent killer of consistency.
challenges.protocol: In bed within ±30 min of target bedtime every night. Auto-verified from Whoop.

challenges.name: Bedroom Detox
challenges.one_liner: Sleep with phone in another room for 7 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Phone proximity disrupts sleep onset
challenges.board_quote: The phone is a dopamine slot machine. It does not belong where you sleep.
challenges.protocol: Phone charges in a different room. Use an alarm clock.

challenges.name: Protein Perfect
challenges.one_liner: Hit 190g protein target every day for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: High protein preserves lean mass during deficit
challenges.board_quote: Adherence is the best diet. But protein adherence is non-negotiable in a deficit.
challenges.protocol: 190g protein minimum per day via MacroFactor.

challenges.name: No DoorDash
challenges.one_liner: Zero food delivery apps for 30 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Food delivery associated with higher caloric intake
challenges.board_quote: DoorDash is the avoidance pattern made frictionless.
challenges.protocol: No DoorDash, UberEats, Grubhub. Cook or eat out in person.

challenges.name: Green With Every Meal
challenges.one_liner: Eat a vegetable with every meal for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Higher vegetable intake reduces all-cause mortality
challenges.board_quote: Micronutrient density starts at the plate.
challenges.protocol: Every meal includes at least one serving of vegetables.

challenges.name: Total Transparency
challenges.one_liner: Log every meal in MacroFactor for 30 straight days
challenges.evidence_summary: Self-monitoring doubles weight loss outcomes
challenges.board_quote: You can't manage what you don't measure. Logging IS the intervention.
challenges.protocol: Every meal logged at time of eating. No 'I'll log it later.'

challenges.name: Home Chef
challenges.one_liner: Cook at home 5+ meals per week for 30 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Home cooking associated with better diet quality
challenges.board_quote: Cooking isn't about perfection — it's about control.
challenges.protocol: Cook at least 5 meals at home per week.

challenges.name: Morning Pages
challenges.one_liner: Journal every morning for 14 days before phone
challenges.evidence_summary: Expressive writing reduces anxiety (Pennebaker, multiple RCTs)
challenges.board_quote: The journal is where you meet yourself before the world tells you who to be today.
challenges.protocol: Write for at least 5 min every morning before any app or email.

challenges.name: Gratitude Practice
challenges.one_liner: Write 3 things you're grateful for every night for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Gratitude journaling improves wellbeing and sleep quality
challenges.board_quote: Gratitude isn't toxic positivity. It's teaching the brain to notice what's already working.
challenges.protocol: Every night, write 3 specific things. Not 'family' but 'the way Brittany laughed at dinner.'

challenges.name: Daily NSDR
challenges.one_liner: 10 minutes of NSDR/meditation daily for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: NSDR improves dopamine by 65%, reduces stress
challenges.board_quote: NSDR is the highest-ROI stress tool. Zero cost, 10 minutes.
challenges.protocol: 10 min of NSDR or body scan meditation daily.

challenges.name: Slow Looking
challenges.one_liner: Visit a museum and sit with one painting for 30+ min
challenges.evidence_summary: Sustained attention to art improves focus, reduces rumination
challenges.board_quote: Truly seeing something without judgment is meditation the anxious mind desperately needs.
challenges.protocol: Go to an art museum. Pick one painting. Sit with it 30+ min. No phone.

challenges.name: Unsent Letter
challenges.one_liner: Write a letter of appreciation to someone
challenges.evidence_summary: Expressing gratitude increases wellbeing for writer and recipient
challenges.board_quote: Vulnerability is the price of admission to meaningful connection.
challenges.protocol: Write a letter to someone who impacted your life. 200+ words. Sending optional.

challenges.name: Digital Detox
challenges.one_liner: No social media for 7 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Social media breaks reduce anxiety and depression
challenges.board_quote: A 7-day reset recalibrates your reward circuitry.
challenges.protocol: Delete social media apps. No IG, X, Reddit, TikTok. Messaging fine.

challenges.name: Read, Don't Scroll
challenges.one_liner: Read 30 min before bed instead of phone for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Reading before bed improves sleep onset latency
challenges.board_quote: A physical book is one of the few wind-down activities that genuinely works.
challenges.protocol: 30 min physical book reading before bed. No backlit e-reader.

challenges.name: Weekly Coffee Date
challenges.one_liner: Coffee with a different friend weekly for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Regular social contact reduces mortality risk by 50%
challenges.board_quote: Social connection is not a luxury. It is the foundation.
challenges.protocol: One in-person coffee/lunch/walk with a friend per week. Different person if possible.

challenges.name: Talk to a Stranger
challenges.one_liner: Brief conversation with a stranger every day for 7 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Brief social interactions boost mood, reduce social anxiety
challenges.board_quote: Every conversation with a stranger is exposure therapy.
challenges.protocol: Initiate a brief exchange. Compliment, question, comment. Min 30 seconds.

challenges.name: Pick Up the Phone
challenges.one_liner: Call (not text) one person per day for 7 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Voice communication creates stronger social bonds than text
challenges.board_quote: A text is a transaction. A phone call is a connection.
challenges.protocol: Call one person per day. Friend, family, or colleague. Must be voice.

challenges.name: Yes Week
challenges.one_liner: Say yes to every social invitation for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Exposure therapy for social anxiety through repeated engagement
challenges.board_quote: Your default is 'no.' Flip the default and watch what happens.
challenges.protocol: Say yes to every social invitation. Only exception: genuine conflict.

challenges.name: Host a Dinner
challenges.one_liner: Plan and host a dinner for 4+ people
challenges.evidence_summary: Communal meals strengthen social bonds
challenges.board_quote: Hosting creates belonging. It doesn't have to be fancy — it has to be real.
challenges.protocol: Host dinner at home for 4+ people. Cook or order — the gathering is the point.

challenges.name: Daily Compliment
challenges.one_liner: Genuine compliment to a stranger every day for 7 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Prosocial behavior increases positive affect
challenges.board_quote: A compliment costs nothing and creates genuine human connection.
challenges.protocol: One genuine face-to-face compliment to a stranger daily. Be specific.

challenges.name: Join Something
challenges.one_liner: Attend a group class/club/meetup weekly for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Group membership reduces mortality risk comparable to quitting smoking
challenges.board_quote: Your social muscle atrophies without group engagement.
challenges.protocol: One group activity per week. Class, club, meetup, sports league. Not the gym.

challenges.name: T0 Perfect Fortnight
challenges.one_liner: Complete all Tier 0 habits for 14 straight days
challenges.evidence_summary: Keystone habit consistency creates identity reinforcement
challenges.board_quote: 14 days of T0 is proof you can close the knowing-doing gap.
challenges.protocol: Every T0 habit every day for 14 consecutive days. One miss resets.

challenges.name: No Snooze
challenges.one_liner: No snooze button for 7 straight days
challenges.evidence_summary: Snoozing fragments sleep architecture
challenges.board_quote: The first decision of the day sets the tone.
challenges.protocol: Alarm goes off, feet on floor. Zero snooze presses.

challenges.name: Cold Finish
challenges.one_liner: End every shower with 30 sec cold for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Cold exposure increases norepinephrine 200-300%
challenges.board_quote: Learning to lean into chosen discomfort transfers to every other domain.
challenges.protocol: End of every shower: coldest setting, 30 seconds minimum.

challenges.name: Make the Bed
challenges.one_liner: Make your bed every morning for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Keystone habit research: small control acts cascade
challenges.board_quote: You can't control the world but you can control your bed.
challenges.protocol: Make bed within 10 min of getting up. Every morning.

challenges.name: 5-Minute Reset
challenges.one_liner: 5-minute tidy-up every evening for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Environmental order reduces cognitive load
challenges.board_quote: Five minutes of tidying is five minutes of reclaiming control.
challenges.protocol: 5-minute timer every evening. Tidy living space. Stop when timer ends.

challenges.name: Lights Out by 10
challenges.one_liner: In bed with lights off by 10pm every night for two weeks
challenges.evidence_summary: Consistent early bedtime aligns with circadian cortisol drop and improves sleep onset latency
challenges.board_quote: The most powerful sleep drug is a consistent bedtime. Nothing else comes close.
challenges.protocol: Lights off by 10:00pm PDT every night. Track with Whoop bedtime metric. 1 grace night allowed.

challenges.name: Noon Caffeine Cutoff
challenges.one_liner: No caffeine after 12pm for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Caffeine half-life is 5-6 hours; afternoon intake elevates adenosine blocking at bedtime
challenges.board_quote: Half-life means half is still circulating. At 6pm you're still 50% caffeinated from a 12pm coffee.
challenges.protocol: No coffee, tea, pre-workout, or caffeine supplements after 12:00pm. Decaf allowed. Track compliance daily.

challenges.name: Cave Mode
challenges.one_liner: Optimize bedroom: blackout, 65°F, no devices for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Cool (65-68°F), dark, quiet sleep environments improve deep sleep % by 15-25%
challenges.board_quote: Your bedroom should be a cave. Cool, dark, boring. That's the entire protocol.
challenges.protocol: Blackout curtains or eye mask, thermostat to 65°F or Eight Sleep cooling, phone charges outside bedroom. Measure deep sleep % change.

challenges.name: The Ritual
challenges.one_liner: Same 30-minute wind-down routine every night for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Consistent pre-sleep routines signal circadian system and reduce sleep onset latency
challenges.board_quote: Routine is a signal. Your body learns 'these actions mean sleep is coming.'
challenges.protocol: Design a 30-min sequence (e.g. stretch, journal, read). Same order, same time, every night. Log compliance.

challenges.name: First Light
challenges.one_liner: Get outside within 30 min of waking for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Morning light exposure anchors circadian clock, improves cortisol awakening response
challenges.board_quote: Huberman's #1: morning sunlight within 30 minutes sets your entire circadian day.
challenges.protocol: Step outside within 30 minutes of waking. 10 min minimum, no sunglasses. Overcast days still count. Track daily.

challenges.name: Heat Therapy
challenges.one_liner: Sauna 3x/week for 20 min at 170°F+ for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Laukkanen et al.: 4-7 sauna sessions/week associated with 40% reduction in all-cause mortality
challenges.board_quote: The Finnish data is staggering. Regular sauna use is one of the strongest longevity signals we have.
challenges.protocol: 3 sessions/week, 170-190°F, 15-20 min. Cool down 5 min between rounds if doing multiple. Hydrate aggressively. Log sessions.

challenges.name: Cold Steel
challenges.one_liner: Cold shower finish (2 min) or cold plunge daily for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Cold exposure increases norepinephrine 200-300%, improves mood, reduces inflammation markers
challenges.board_quote: The discomfort IS the mechanism. Deliberate cold trains your stress response system.
challenges.protocol: End every shower with 2+ minutes of cold (as cold as it goes), OR cold plunge 50-59°F for 2 min. Morning preferred. Log daily.

challenges.name: Couch to 5K
challenges.one_liner: Complete a Couch to 5K program (8 weeks)
challenges.evidence_summary: Progressive running programs build aerobic base safely; VO2max improvement 15-20%
challenges.board_quote: The evidence for running and all-cause mortality is bulletproof. Getting to a 5K is the single highest ROI fitness goal.
challenges.protocol: Follow structured C25K plan (3 runs/week, walk-run intervals progressing to continuous). Track with Strava. Target: run 5K without stopping.

challenges.name: Zone 2 Machine
challenges.one_liner: Hit 150 min/week of Zone 2 cardio for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: WHO + Attia: 150 min/week moderate cardio is the longevity minimum. Zone 2 specifically improves mitochondrial function.
challenges.board_quote: Zone 2 is boring. It's also the single most important exercise modality for longevity.
challenges.protocol: 150+ minutes per week in HR Zone 2 (roughly 60-70% max HR). Can be walking, cycling, rowing, swimming. Track via Whoop/Strava.

challenges.name: Limber Up
challenges.one_liner: 15 min mobility/stretching every day for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Daily mobility work reduces injury risk, improves recovery, maintains joint range of motion
challenges.board_quote: Flexibility is the pillar people skip. It catches up with you after 40.
challenges.protocol: 15 minutes daily: hip openers, thoracic spine, hamstrings, shoulders. Can be yoga, foam rolling, or structured routine. Track compliance.

challenges.name: Post-Meal Walk
challenges.one_liner: 10-minute walk within 30 min of every meal for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Post-meal walking reduces glucose spike by 30-50% (CGM data); improves insulin sensitivity
challenges.board_quote: The cheapest glucose drug in the world is a 10-minute walk after dinner.
challenges.protocol: Walk 10+ minutes within 30 min of each main meal. All 3 meals ideal, minimum 1/day (dinner). Track with steps + CGM correlation.

challenges.name: Grip of Steel
challenges.one_liner: Dead hangs + farmer carries 3x/week for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Grip strength is the single strongest predictor of all-cause mortality in exercise science
challenges.board_quote: If you can only measure one physical attribute for longevity prediction, measure grip strength.
challenges.protocol: 3x/week: 3 sets max dead hang + 2 sets farmer carry (heavy, 30 sec). Track hang time progression.

challenges.name: Sugar Free
challenges.one_liner: No added sugar for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Added sugar drives insulin resistance, inflammation, and triglyceride elevation
challenges.board_quote: Two weeks without added sugar resets your palate and your insulin sensitivity.
challenges.protocol: No foods with added sugar in ingredients. Whole fruit OK. Read every label. Track compliance + CGM response.

challenges.name: Time-Restricted Eating
challenges.one_liner: 16:8 intermittent fasting for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: 16:8 TRE improves insulin sensitivity, may reduce inflammation markers; mixed evidence on weight loss vs caloric restriction alone
challenges.board_quote: The evidence is nuanced. TRE probably helps via calorie control and circadian alignment, not magic.
challenges.protocol: Eating window 12pm-8pm (or similar 8-hour window). Water, black coffee, tea allowed outside. Track window compliance.

challenges.name: Prep Day
challenges.one_liner: Meal prep every Sunday for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Meal prepping correlates with higher diet quality, lower impulse eating, and reduced food delivery reliance
challenges.board_quote: DoorDash is the enemy. Sunday prep is the fortress.
challenges.protocol: Every Sunday: prep 4+ meals for the week. Protein + veg + complex carb. Photograph and log. Track DoorDash orders (target: zero).

challenges.name: Water Works
challenges.one_liner: Drink 100oz of water daily for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Adequate hydration improves cognitive function, exercise performance, and satiety signals
challenges.board_quote: Most people are chronically mildly dehydrated. It affects everything from focus to hunger.
challenges.protocol: 100oz (3L) water daily. Coffee/tea don't count. Use marked water bottle. Log intake.

challenges.name: Veggies First
challenges.one_liner: Eat vegetables before carbs at every meal for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Eating fiber before carbohydrates reduces glucose spike by 30-40% (Jesse Inchauspé / CGM studies)
challenges.board_quote: Same meal, different order, dramatically different glucose response. The data is clear.
challenges.protocol: At every meal with carbs, eat the vegetables and protein first, carbs last. Track with CGM.

challenges.name: Box Breathing
challenges.one_liner: 5 min box breathing daily for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Cyclic sighing / box breathing reduces cortisol and improves HRV within a single session (Huberman Lab)
challenges.board_quote: Five minutes of structured breathing does more for acute stress than any supplement.
challenges.protocol: 5 min daily: 4-count inhale, 4-count hold, 4-count exhale, 4-count hold. Morning or before bed. Track HRV response.

challenges.name: Structured Journal
challenges.one_liner: Write in a structured CBT-style journal daily for 21 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Expressive writing and structured journaling reduce anxiety and depressive symptoms (Pennebaker, multiple RCTs)
challenges.board_quote: Journaling isn't therapy. But it uses the same mechanism: externalizing thought patterns makes them examinable.
challenges.protocol: Daily 10-min journal using format: Situation → Thought → Emotion → Evidence For/Against → Reframe. Track mood before/after.

challenges.name: 10 Minutes Still
challenges.one_liner: 10 min meditation daily for 30 days
challenges.evidence_summary: 8-week meditation programs show measurable changes in amygdala reactivity and prefrontal cortex thickness
challenges.board_quote: The evidence base for meditation is now enormous. 10 minutes daily is the minimum effective dose.
challenges.protocol: 10 min daily: guided (Waking Up, Headspace) or unguided. Same time each day preferred. Track streak and subjective calm rating.

challenges.name: Forest Bath
challenges.one_liner: 30 min in nature (no phone) 3x/week for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Shinrin-yoku research: 30 min in nature reduces cortisol 12-16%, lowers blood pressure, improves NK cell activity
challenges.board_quote: The Japanese have known this for decades. Nature exposure is a legitimate clinical intervention.
challenges.protocol: 3x/week: 30+ min outdoors in green space. Phone on airplane mode. Walk, sit, or hike. Log location and subjective mood shift.

challenges.name: Worry Window
challenges.one_liner: Contain all worrying to a 15-min daily window for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Stimulus control for worry (scheduled worry time) reduces generalized anxiety symptoms (CBT literature)
challenges.board_quote: Paradoxically, giving yourself permission to worry at a set time reduces total worry time.
challenges.protocol: Pick a 15-min window each day (not before bed). Write all worries during that window. Outside the window, defer: 'I'll worry about that at 5pm.' Track worry frequency outside window.

challenges.name: Present Meals
challenges.one_liner: No phones at the dinner table for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Phone presence reduces conversation depth and empathy even when not in use (Przybylski & Weinstein, 2013)
challenges.board_quote: The phone on the table is a third person in the conversation. Remove it.
challenges.protocol: Phone stays in another room during dinner. Both partners/all household members commit. Track compliance and conversation quality.

challenges.name: Reach Back
challenges.one_liner: Contact one old friend you've lost touch with each week for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Weak ties reactivation improves wellbeing and social network resilience (Granovetter + Murthy)
challenges.board_quote: The friends you've drifted from aren't gone. One text reopens the door.
challenges.protocol: One text/call per week to someone you haven't spoken to in 3+ months. No agenda, just reconnecting. Log the interaction.

challenges.name: Open Up
challenges.one_liner: Share something vulnerable with someone you trust once a week for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Vulnerability in relationships deepens trust and connection (Brené Brown research; Murthy loneliness framework)
challenges.board_quote: Depth of connection matters more than frequency. One vulnerable conversation outweighs ten surface ones.
challenges.protocol: Once per week: share something real (a fear, a struggle, a hope) with someone you trust. Can be in person, phone, or letter. Journal about it after.

challenges.name: Find Your Tribe
challenges.one_liner: Attend the same group/class/meetup weekly for a month
challenges.evidence_summary: Regular group participation is the strongest predictor of social wellbeing (Murthy, Holt-Lunstad meta-analysis)
challenges.board_quote: Belonging requires showing up. Not once. Repeatedly. Same place, same people.
challenges.protocol: Pick one: running club, gym class, book club, volunteer group, church, coding meetup. Attend weekly, same time. Track attendance.

challenges.name: Daily Thanks
challenges.one_liner: Send one genuine gratitude text per day for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Expressing gratitude improves both sender and receiver wellbeing; strengthens social bonds (Emmons & McCullough)
challenges.board_quote: This is the highest ROI social habit. 30 seconds, one text, two people feel better.
challenges.protocol: One text per day to a different person: specific, genuine thanks for something they did or are. Not generic. Track who and what.

challenges.name: Clear Mind
challenges.one_liner: No cannabis for 30 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Cannabis disrupts REM sleep architecture; 30-day abstinence restores sleep stages and improves motivation
challenges.board_quote: REM is where emotional processing happens. Cannabis suppresses it. The dreams come back — that's the healing.
challenges.protocol: Zero cannabis in any form for 30 days. Track sleep quality, dream recall, motivation, and cravings daily. Use vice streak tracker.

challenges.name: Eyes Forward
challenges.one_liner: No pornography for 30 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Pornography abstinence associated with improved motivation, reduced compulsive behavior, and restored dopamine sensitivity
challenges.board_quote: The dopamine circuit doesn't care about the source. Superstimulus withdrawal restores baseline.
challenges.protocol: Zero pornography in any form for 30 days. Note triggers, use redirect strategies. Track via vice streak. Journal urges.

challenges.name: Clockwork
challenges.one_liner: Wake at the exact same time (±15 min) for 21 days — weekends included
challenges.evidence_summary: Consistent wake time is the single strongest circadian anchor; social jet lag disrupts metabolic health
challenges.board_quote: Your body doesn't know it's Saturday. Social jet lag is real jet lag.
challenges.protocol: Set one wake time. Hit it within 15 min every day including weekends. No snooze. Track with Whoop wake time.

challenges.name: Dopamine Reset
challenges.one_liner: No social media, video games, or streaming for 7 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Temporary abstinence from high-dopamine stimuli restores baseline sensitivity and improves capacity for delayed gratification
challenges.board_quote: Seven days without superstimuli makes everything else feel interesting again. Books, walks, conversations.
challenges.protocol: Delete social media apps, no YouTube/TikTok/Netflix/gaming for 7 days. News OK via text only. Replace with books, walks, conversation. Journal daily.

challenges.name: Kitchen Only
challenges.one_liner: Only eat while seated at the kitchen table for 14 days
challenges.evidence_summary: Mindful eating location reduces caloric intake by removing distraction-based overconsumption
challenges.board_quote: Eating at your desk or on the couch is invisible consumption. The kitchen table makes every bite intentional.
challenges.protocol: All meals and snacks consumed seated at the kitchen/dining table. No eating at desk, in car, on couch, or standing. Track compliance.

challenges.name: Photon Protocol
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="20" cy="20" r="6" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="20" y1="4" x2="20" y2="9" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="20" y1="31" x2="20" y2="36" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="4" y1="20" x2="9" y2="20" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="31" y1="20" x2="36" y2="20" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="8.1" y1="8.1" x2="11.8" y2="11.8" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="28.2" y1="28.2" x2="31.9" y2="31.9" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="31.9" y1="8.1" x2="28.2" y2="11.8" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="11.8" y1="28.2" x2="8.1" y2="31.9" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: 10 min morning light. No excuses, no weather clause.
challenges.evidence_summary: Morning light anchors the cortisol awakening response, calibrates circadian timing, and cascades into HRV, sleep architecture, and metabolic rate. Free. Zero cost.
challenges.board_quote: Morning sunlight is the single highest-ROI protocol. You missed it 70% of the time. That ends now.
challenges.protocol: Within 30 minutes of waking, 10 minutes of outdoor light or Luminette glasses. Log it before 9am.

challenges.name: The 9:30 Protocol
challenges.one_liner: Phone away at 8:15. Lights out at 9. Asleep by 9:30.
challenges.evidence_summary: Sleep onset consistency is the strongest predictor of sleep architecture quality. Even one standard deviation of timing variance tanks REM% and suppresses HRV recovery.
challenges.board_quote: Your HRV dropped 33% this month. Architecture beats duration. The runway starts at 8:15.
challenges.protocol: Phone physically out of reach by 8:15pm. Lights dimmed by 8:30pm. Horizontal, lights out by 9:00pm. Target asleep by 9:30pm.

challenges.name: Analog Hour
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="20" cy="20" r="14" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.75"/><line x1="20" y1="7.5" x2="20" y2="11" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="20" y1="29" x2="20" y2="32.5" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="7.5" y1="20" x2="11" y2="20" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="29" y1="20" x2="32.5" y2="20" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="20" y1="20" x2="14.5" y2="13.5" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="20" y1="20" x2="26" y2="14.5" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/><circle cx="20" cy="20" r="1.75" fill="#1D9E75"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: One screen-free hour. Any hour. No algorithm.
challenges.evidence_summary: Voluntary sensory decompression without algorithm-driven engagement lowers ambient cortisol, restores directed attention, and creates the processing window where emotional integration occurs.
challenges.board_quote: This is self-companionship. Sitting with yourself without distraction is harder than it sounds. Start with one hour.
challenges.protocol: One hour per day — any hour — with zero screens. Walk, cook, sketch, sit outside, read physical pages. Log the hour and what you did with it.

challenges.name: No Doom Scroll Window
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="11" y="4" width="18" height="30" rx="3" fill="#F1EFE8" stroke="#5F5E5A" stroke-width="1.75"/><line x1="15" y1="11" x2="25" y2="11" stroke="#5F5E5A" stroke-width="1.2" opacity="0.4"/><line x1="15" y1="15.5" x2="25" y2="15.5" stroke="#5F5E5A" stroke-width="1.2" opacity="0.4"/><line x1="15" y1="20" x2="22" y2="20" stroke="#5F5E5A" stroke-width="1.2" opacity="0.4"/><circle cx="20" cy="30" r="1.5" fill="#5F5E5A" opacity="0.5"/><path d="M27.5 26.5 A 6 6 0 1 0 27.5 32.5 A 4.5 4.5 0 0 1 27.5 26.5 Z" fill="#444441"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: No feeds after 8pm. Podcasts fine. Feeds are not.
challenges.evidence_summary: Evening feed consumption compresses sleep pressure, mildly elevates cortisol, and — critically — fills the quiet window where the brain processes the day. That window matters.
challenges.board_quote: The quiet time isn't empty. It's where the day actually gets digested. Protect it.
challenges.protocol: No phone-based content feeds (news, social, video) between 8pm and wake time. Podcasts, music, calls, texts allowed. Log compliance daily.

challenges.name: Reddit Blackout
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><ellipse cx="20" cy="23" rx="11.5" ry="10" fill="#FCEBEB" stroke="#E24B4A" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="15.5" cy="22" r="2" fill="#E24B4A"/><circle cx="24.5" cy="22" r="2" fill="#E24B4A"/><path d="M15.5 27.5 Q20 30.5 24.5 27.5" stroke="#E24B4A" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/><line x1="16" y1="13.5" x2="13" y2="8" stroke="#E24B4A" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/><circle cx="12" cy="7" r="2" fill="#E24B4A"/><line x1="24" y1="13.5" x2="27" y2="8" stroke="#E24B4A" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/><circle cx="28" cy="7" r="2" fill="#E24B4A"/><circle cx="20" cy="20" r="17" stroke="#A32D2D" stroke-width="2.5"/><line x1="8" y1="8" x2="32" y2="32" stroke="#A32D2D" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: Delete the app. 7 days. Reinstall if you want on day 8.
challenges.evidence_summary: Variable-ratio reinforcement loops (Reddit's scroll model) suppress dopamine baseline by training the brain to seek micro-rewards constantly. A 7-day break measurably resets tonic dopamine levels.
challenges.board_quote: Motivation follows action. But first you have to clear the noise that's substituting for it.
challenges.protocol: Delete Reddit from phone for 7 days. Desktop research use fine. Leisure scroll is not. First 5 minutes of any urge: physical action — walk to another room, glass of water, open a book.

challenges.name: Fiction First
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M20 13 L8 17 L8 32 L20 28 Z" fill="#EEEDFE" stroke="#534AB7" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linejoin="round"/><path d="M20 13 L32 17 L32 32 L20 28 Z" fill="#EEEDFE" stroke="#534AB7" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linejoin="round"/><line x1="20" y1="13" x2="20" y2="28" stroke="#534AB7" stroke-width="1.75"/><line x1="10.5" y1="21.5" x2="17.5" y2="20.5" stroke="#7F77DD" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6"/><line x1="10.5" y1="24.5" x2="17.5" y2="23.5" stroke="#7F77DD" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6"/><path d="M20 7 L21.2 9.5 L23.5 9.5 L21.8 11 L22.5 13.5 L20 12 L17.5 13.5 L18.2 11 L16.5 9.5 L18.8 9.5 Z" fill="#534AB7"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: 20 min fiction before any self-help. Every day.
challenges.evidence_summary: Fiction uniquely activates theory of mind and narrative imagination — cognitive modes that problem-solving and self-help content suppress. For someone in isolation, fiction is practice inhabiting another's inner life.
challenges.board_quote: Fiction is the one context where you're allowed to fully inhabit someone else's inner life. That's not entertainment. That's practice.
challenges.protocol: 20 minutes of fiction (book, audiobook, Kindle) before any non-fiction, self-help, or productivity content. Rule is sequence, not duration. Log the title.

challenges.name: The Daily Verdict
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="20" y1="9" x2="20" y2="33" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="14" y1="33" x2="26" y2="33" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="7" y1="16" x2="33" y2="16" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="10" y1="16" x2="10" y2="22" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M7 22 Q10 26.5 13 22" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/><line x1="30" y1="16" x2="30" y2="22" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M27 22 Q30 26.5 33 22" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: Two sentences. What went well. What's next.
challenges.evidence_summary: Self-esteem is built from accurate self-perception, not performance or praise. Two constrained sentences prevent rumination while building the evidence base of clear self-seeing.
challenges.board_quote: The most underrated challenge on this entire list. You already journal. This focuses it.
challenges.protocol: Each evening before 9pm: exactly two sentences in your journal. One on what you did well (not perfectly — well). One on what you want differently tomorrow. No more, no less.

challenges.name: NSDR Reset
challenges.one_liner: 10-20 min non-sleep deep rest. Every afternoon.
challenges.evidence_summary: NSDR (Yoga Nidra protocol) triggers parasympathetic dominance without crossing into sleep staging. The fastest zero-cost intervention for autonomic dysregulation. HRV 29.56 — this is indicated now.
challenges.board_quote: Your recovery score is 40. NSDR is the fastest zero-cost reversal. There is no downside.
challenges.protocol: 1pm–3pm daily: 10–20 minutes Yoga Nidra script (free on YouTube), eyes closed, horizontal. Not napping. Log each session.

challenges.name: The Depth Rep
challenges.one_liner: Once a week, say the thing you'd normally not say.
challenges.evidence_summary: Murthy: vulnerability is the price of admission to meaningful connection. Surface-level contact doesn't meet the social connection threshold for wellbeing. Depth requires practice, not talent.
challenges.board_quote: You can spend 150 hours in shallow proximity. The challenge is depth, not quantity.
challenges.protocol: Once per week, in a conversation with someone you care about, say one thing you'd normally hold back. Not a confession — one degree deeper than comfortable. Log what you said (private).

challenges.name: The Ten-Day Letter
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="4" y="11" width="32" height="22" rx="2" fill="#FAECE7" stroke="#D85A30" stroke-width="1.75"/><path d="M4 11 L20 23 L36 11" stroke="#D85A30" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" fill="none"/><line x1="4" y1="33" x2="15" y2="23" stroke="#D85A30" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.35"/><line x1="36" y1="33" x2="25" y2="23" stroke="#D85A30" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.35"/><circle cx="20" cy="23" r="3.5" fill="#D85A30" opacity="0.75"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: Write it. Don't send it. Before April 1st.
challenges.evidence_summary: Expressive writing about avoidance periods reduces their psychological grip and creates the narrative distance needed to understand what was actually happening rather than just that it happened.
challenges.board_quote: Before you build the platform further, you need to know what you actually needed in March. Write the letter.
challenges.protocol: One unsent, private letter to March 17th-Matthew. Three paragraphs: what happened, what you needed that you didn't give yourself, what you want April-Matthew to know. Store in journal. Don't share.

challenges.name: Zone 2 Foundation Block
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M20 34 C20 34 5 24 5 15 C5 10.5 8.5 7 13 7 C15.5 7 18 8.5 20 11 C22 8.5 24.5 7 27 7 C31.5 7 35 10.5 35 15 C35 24 20 34 20 34 Z" fill="#FCEBEB" stroke="#E24B4A" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linejoin="round"/><polyline points="6,19.5 10,19.5 12.5,14 15,24 17.5,17 20,21 22,19.5 34,19.5" stroke="#E24B4A" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" fill="none"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: 4x Zone 2 sessions per week. 30–45 min. Heart rate under 140.
challenges.evidence_summary: CTL 4.45 means no accumulated fitness base. ACWR 0.26 means any intensity spike represents a 4x load jump. Zone 2 builds aerobic base safely and adds ~5 CTL over 3 weeks.
challenges.board_quote: You can't buy fitness in a week of intensity. Zone 2 is the foundation everything else sits on.
challenges.protocol: 4 Zone 2 sessions/week, 30–45 min each, HR 120–140 BPM. Walk, bike, elliptical — your call. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too hard. Log every session.

challenges.name: 10-Day Walk Streak
challenges.one_liner: 5,000+ steps. 10 consecutive days. No rest days.
challenges.evidence_summary: Walk 5k was missed 14 of 20 tracked days — the single most missed Tier 0 habit. When the walk goes, everything cascades. This is the canary. Fix the foundation.
challenges.board_quote: The walk is the canary. When it goes, everything goes. You've proven that now.
challenges.protocol: 5,000+ steps every single day for 10 consecutive days. No exceptions. Track in Apple Health or Garmin.

challenges.name: Protein Anchor Protocol
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="20" cy="9.5" r="3.5" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75"/><line x1="20" y1="13" x2="20" y2="33" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="11" y1="17" x2="29" y2="17" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M20 33 L9 27.5" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M9 27.5 Q8.5 23 11.5 23" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/><path d="M20 33 L31 27.5" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M31 27.5 Q31.5 23 28.5 23" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/><path d="M9 27.5 Q20 36.5 31 27.5" stroke="#378ADD" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: Every meal hits 35g protein minimum. Pre-plan each morning.
challenges.evidence_summary: When protein is consistent, total calories self-regulate. At a 1500 kcal deficit, protein protection is non-negotiable. Anchoring protein first reduces total dietary decision fatigue.
challenges.board_quote: Build from what's working. Protein distribution matters as much as total protein.
challenges.protocol: Every meal hits 35g protein minimum. Each morning, 2 minutes: pre-plan the day's protein sources before the day starts. Log in MacroFactor.

challenges.name: No-Drift Weekends
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="5" y="10" width="30" height="26" rx="3" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="1.75"/><rect x="5" y="10" width="30" height="8" rx="2" fill="#EAF3DE" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="1.75"/><line x1="13" y1="7" x2="13" y2="13" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="27" y1="7" x2="27" y2="13" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/><rect x="21.5" y="21.5" width="10" height="9" rx="2" fill="#EAF3DE" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="1.25"/><path d="M23.5 25.5 L25.5 27.5 L29.5 22.5" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" fill="none"/><line x1="8.5" y1="23.5" x2="17" y2="23.5" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="1.25" opacity="0.45"/><line x1="8.5" y1="27" x2="17" y2="27" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="1.25" opacity="0.45"/><line x1="8.5" y1="30.5" x2="14" y2="30.5" stroke="#639922" stroke-width="1.25" opacity="0.3"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: Log first meal within 30 min of breaking your fast. Both weekend days.
challenges.evidence_summary: Calorie goal was missed 13 of 20 tracked days. The weekend reset pattern is structural, not motivational. Removing the anchor-point delay is the highest-leverage intervention.
challenges.board_quote: You're losing 5 lbs a month even with the drift. Imagine what you lose if you actually hold weekends.
challenges.protocol: Saturday and Sunday: log first meal within 30 min of breaking fast (11:30am IF window). All subsequent meals logged same-day. No morning logging required — IF window respected.

challenges.name: Minimum Viable Day
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="5" y="4" width="9" height="9" rx="2" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.75"/><path d="M7 8.5 L9 10.5 L13 5.5" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><line x1="18" y1="8.5" x2="35" y2="8.5" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="5" y="17" width="9" height="9" rx="2" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.75"/><path d="M7 21.5 L9 23.5 L13 18.5" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><line x1="18" y1="21.5" x2="35" y2="21.5" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="5" y="30" width="9" height="9" rx="2" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.75" opacity="0.35"/><line x1="18" y1="34.5" x2="30" y2="34.5" stroke="#1D9E75" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.2"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: Walk. Log food. No alcohol. Three things. Day won.
challenges.evidence_summary: After a 10-day complete zero period, the urge to overcommit is the exact pattern that creates the next crash. MVD builds the evidence base that the bar is still there — without requiring heroics.
challenges.board_quote: The goal isn't a lowered bar. It's proving to your nervous system that the bar still exists.
challenges.protocol: For 14 days, win the day by completing exactly three things: Walk 5k steps. Log food (even imperfectly). No alcohol. Everything else is a bonus. 12 of 14 days required.

challenges.name: The Discomfort Rep
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="20" y1="5" x2="20" y2="35" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="5" y1="20" x2="35" y2="20" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="9.4" y1="9.4" x2="30.6" y2="30.6" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="30.6" y1="9.4" x2="9.4" y2="30.6" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="20" y1="10.5" x2="16.5" y2="7" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="20" y1="10.5" x2="23.5" y2="7" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="20" y1="29.5" x2="16.5" y2="33" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="20" y1="29.5" x2="23.5" y2="33" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="10.5" y1="20" x2="7" y2="16.5" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="10.5" y1="20" x2="7" y2="23.5" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="29.5" y1="20" x2="33" y2="16.5" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="29.5" y1="20" x2="33" y2="23.5" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><circle cx="20" cy="20" r="3" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: One chosen discomfort, daily. Log what you chose.
challenges.evidence_summary: Discomfort tolerance correlates directly with dopamine baseline. People with higher tolerance don't feel more pleasure — they require less stimulus to feel it. Each rep is a vote for the identity: I do hard things.
challenges.board_quote: Effort before reward. That's the sequence that rewires the motivational architecture.
challenges.protocol: Once per day, one mildly uncomfortable act on purpose: cold shower 90s, take stairs, eat the planned meal when easier is available, have a hard conversation. Log what you chose (one line).

challenges.name: The Hard Thing First
challenges.icon_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="5" y="24" width="30" height="6" rx="2" fill="#FAEEDA" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M20 8 L20 23" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M20 8 L14.5 14.5" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2.25" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M20 8 L25.5 14.5" stroke="#EF9F27" stroke-width="2.25" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg>
challenges.one_liner: Most avoided task, every morning. Before anything else.
challenges.evidence_summary: Avoidance override is a learnable skill. 10 days of doing the most avoided task first builds the evidence base that avoidance can be overridden — the psychological muscle everything else depends on.
challenges.board_quote: The gap between knowing and doing is where the real work lives. This is the practice.
challenges.protocol: Each morning: identify the single most avoided (not most urgent) task. Complete it before anything else. Log what it was in one line.
